text.title: schedule-development Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for schedule-development as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Padding individual activity durations instead of using project-end and feeding buffers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Per-task padding hides slack, triggers Parkinson's Law (work expands to fill allotted time), and makes the critical path invisible.

failure-mode.description: Reporting only "the critical path" as a single chain when multiple paths tie at the same finish.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multi-path criticality means all paths with float less than or equal to 0 must be managed; reporting only one gives false security.

failure-mode.description: LLM produces wildly optimistic durations because training data emphasises happy paths.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force three-point estimation with explicit pessimistic values from the project team; reject point estimates for uncertain activities.
